title: A Nurse Gets a Special Retirement Send-Off

source.title: Nurse who went above and beyond gets heartfelt send-off: "You're an angel"

tags: CBS Evening News / On the Road

segments.title: Last Day
segments.audioText: When Wynola Wayne walked out of work for the last time after 58 years as a nurse, you could tell this was no ordinary retirement send-off.
segments.transcript: When Wynola Wayne walked out of work for the last time after 58 years as a nurse, you could tell this was no ordinary retirement send-off.
segments.translationZh: Wynola Wayne 做咗 58 年護士，最後一次行出工作地方嗰刻，你會睇得出呢個唔係普通退休送別。

points.label: walked out of work
points.explanationZh: walked out of work 即『離開工作地方』；呢度係講最後一日收工。

points.label: for the last time
points.explanationZh: 呢個 chunk 表示『最後一次』，聽到 last time 要留意情緒同背景。

points.label: no ordinary retirement send-off
points.explanationZh: no ordinary 即『唔普通』；retirement send-off 係退休送別。

segments.title: Above and Beyond
segments.audioText: This was the canonization of a saint who went above and beyond for her patients.
segments.transcript: This was the canonization of a saint who went above and beyond for her patients.
segments.translationZh: 呢個送別好似係為一位聖人加冕，因為佢為病人付出遠超本分。

points.label: canonization of a saint
points.explanationZh: canonization 原意係封聖；新聞用誇張講法，突出大家好尊敬佢。

points.label: went above and beyond
points.explanationZh: 固定講法，意思係做多過應份嘅事，常用嚟讚人好盡責。

points.label: for her patients
points.explanationZh: for 帶出受益對象；patients 即病人。

segments.title: The Fire
segments.audioText: Back in 1965, Marco Houpe, who was just 15 months old at the time, was visiting his grandma's house when a Christmas tree caught fire.
segments.transcript: Back in 1965, Marco Houpe, who was just 15 months old at the time, was visiting his grandma's house when a Christmas tree caught fire.
segments.translationZh: 返到 1965 年，Marco Houpe 當時得 15 個月大，去嫲嫲屋企時，一棵聖誕樹起火。

points.label: Back in 1965
points.explanationZh: Back in... 用嚟帶你返去過去某個時間。

points.label: 15 months old at the time
points.explanationZh: at the time 即『當時』；聽年齡資料時要抓數字。

points.label: caught fire
points.explanationZh: caught fire 係固定講法，意思係著火。

segments.title: The Hospital
segments.audioText: Houpe was brought to what is now Nationwide Children's Hospital in Columbus, Ohio.
segments.transcript: Houpe was brought to what is now Nationwide Children's Hospital in Columbus, Ohio.
segments.translationZh: Houpe 被送到而家叫 Nationwide Children's Hospital、位於俄亥俄州哥倫布市嘅醫院。

points.label: was brought to
points.explanationZh: 被動式 was brought to 即『被送到』，新聞常用嚟講病人或傷者去到邊度。

points.label: what is now
points.explanationZh: what is now 表示『而家叫做／而家係』，用嚟補充現時名稱。

points.label: in Columbus, Ohio
points.explanationZh: 城市加州名係美國新聞常見地點格式。

segments.title: Life Today
segments.audioText: They have two children, and Houpe has a good job as a school administrator and coach.
segments.transcript: They have two children, and Houpe has a good job as a school administrator and coach.
segments.translationZh: 佢哋有兩個小朋友，而 Houpe 亦有一份好工作，做學校行政人員同教練。

points.label: They have two children
points.explanationZh: 簡單 present tense，講而家嘅家庭情況。

points.label: has a good job as
points.explanationZh: has a job as... 即『做……工作』，好實用。

points.label: administrator and coach
points.explanationZh: administrator 係行政人員；coach 係教練。

sentences: When Wynola Wayne walked out of work for the last time after 58 years as a nurse, you could tell this was no ordinary retirement send-off.
This was the canonization of a saint who went above and beyond for her patients.
Back in 1965, Marco Houpe, who was just 15 months old at the time, was visiting his grandma's house when a Christmas tree caught fire.
Houpe was brought to what is now Nationwide Children's Hospital in Columbus, Ohio.
They have two children, and Houpe has a good job as a school administrator and coach.

vocabulary.example: Her retirement send-off was full of love.
vocabulary.translationZh: 佢嘅退休送別充滿愛同感謝。

vocabulary.example: The team planned a send-off for their coworker.
vocabulary.translationZh: 團隊為同事安排咗一個歡送會。

vocabulary.example: The nurse cared for every patient with kindness.
vocabulary.translationZh: 護士好有善意咁照顧每位病人。

vocabulary.example: This was no ordinary day at work.
He was visiting his grandma's house.
He works as a school administrator.
She is a coach after school.

phrases.example: She walked out of work for the last time.
She went above and beyond for her patients.
He has a good job as a coach.

speakingPractice.promptZh: 由 00:00 開始跟讀第一句，先分成 When Wynola Wayne... / for the last time / after 58 years as a nurse。
重播 00:10，聽寫 went above and beyond for her patients，再對稿改正。
用 no ordinary 練一句：This was no ordinary day / no ordinary party / no ordinary thank-you.
用兩句英文講返重點：Wynola Wayne worked as a nurse for 58 years. Her patients gave her a special send-off.

notes: 今日片段係 CBS Evening News 約 2 分 54 秒嘅 On the Road 故事，主題係一位護士退休時得到特別送別。
聽力重點係故事時間線：last day、1965、hospital、life today。
跟讀時留意 went above and beyond 呢個常用讚人 phrase，日常同工作場合都好實用。
dailyLifePractice 會練感謝、安排簡單歡送、同請同事幫手，語氣保持自然同低壓。

dailyLifePractice.title: Thanking Someone at Work
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 同事幫咗你一個忙，你想自然咁講多謝，唔好太正式。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Thanks for helping me out today. I really appreciate it. No problem. Happy to help. You went above and beyond.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Thanks for helping me out today.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Thanks for helping me out 比 Thank you for your assistance 輕鬆自然好多。You went above and beyond 係真心讚人，適合對方做多咗好多時用。

drills.label: quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 I really appreciate it. 三次，語氣真誠但放鬆。

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 用 Thanks for... 換成三件事。
drills.example: Thanks for covering my shift today.

dailyLifePractice.title: Planning a Simple Send-Off
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同同事想為即將離職或退休嘅人安排一個簡單歡送。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Should we do a small send-off for Maya? Yeah, let's keep it simple. I can grab a card. Great. I'll ask everyone to sign it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Should we do a small send-off for Maya?
lines.zh: 我哋要唔要為 Maya 搞個小小歡送？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Great. I'll ask everyone to sign it.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: grab a card 係好口語嘅講法，意思係順手買張卡。Let's keep it simple 令人覺得安排輕鬆，唔會變成大工程。

drills.label: role-play
drills.prompt: 你做 A，提議為某人搞簡單歡送。
drills.example: Should we do a small send-off for Chris?

drills.label: say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 I can grab... 講你可以負責嘅一樣嘢。

dailyLifePractice.title: Covering for a Coworker
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 同事需要早走，你想簡短確認自己可以幫手頂一頂。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I need to head out a little early today. Can you cover the front desk for ten minutes? Sure, I've got it. Thanks, I owe you one.

lines.speaker: Coworker
lines.en: I need to head out a little early today.

lines.speaker: Coworker
lines.en: Can you cover the front desk for ten minutes?
lines.zh: 你可唔可以幫我睇前台十分鐘？

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: I've got it 即『我搞得掂／交畀我』，好短好自然。I owe you one 係口語，表示下次會還人情。

drills.label: short response practice
drills.prompt: 對方請你幫十分鐘，你用一句自然答應。

drills.label: polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 Can you cover...? 換成其他小任務。
drills.example: Can you cover the phones for five minutes?
